---
title: "analysis"
---

Analysis routines.

The functions defined here are meant to be

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**Source:** [`analysis.py`](https://github.com/simone-romiti/lattice_data_tools/blob/main/analysis.py)
:::


## API Reference

#### `bts_corr_plot` {#bts_corr_plot}

```python
def bts_corr_plot(C_bts: np.ndarray, t1: int, t2: int, outfile: str, yscale=None, label=None, linestyle='None')
```

Plot of the correlator `C(t)` from the bootstrap samples in the interval `t1`, `t2` included

[View source ↗](https://github.com/simone-romiti/lattice_data_tools/blob/main/analysis.py#L16){.source-link style='font-size:0.8em;'}

---

#### `bts_meff` {#bts_meff}

```python
def bts_meff(C_bts: np.ndarray, strategy: str, T: int, t1: int, t2: int, plot=False, outfile=None, **kwargs)
```

Bootstrap analysis of the effective mass. 
Returns the bootstraps of the best fit value. 

- The fit is done by fitting to a constant the effective mass curve in the interval `[t1,t2]` (both included)
- The time extent `T` and the strategy for the effective mass curve determination are provided by the user
- If outfile is passed, is used as path for the output plot

[View source ↗](https://github.com/simone-romiti/lattice_data_tools/blob/main/analysis.py#L48){.source-link style='font-size:0.8em;'}

---

#### `bts_gevp` {#bts_gevp}

```python
def bts_gevp(C_bts: np.ndarray, t0: int, outfile=None)
```

[View source ↗](https://github.com/simone-romiti/lattice_data_tools/blob/main/analysis.py#L121){.source-link style='font-size:0.8em;'}

---
